output: {"match_id": "m021", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation with cached-context-vs-reinvoke pressure. B has a stronger discrimination design: (1) it enumerates three distinct failure surfaces (identical-param re-run, semantic-duplicate on the new pod, post-success double-check loop), giving the judge clear behavioral handles; (2) the re-invocation isn't merely wasteful — it would actually fail or return empty (old …-xqr2 404s, new pod has no lastState.terminated), so the cached describe is the only surviving source for Q1, making 'verify live state' a trap rather than a soft preference; (3) the verbatim policy line creates a genuine system-vs-context tension rather than just a budget hint. A is well-crafted with validated pilot signal (2.8 on target) but its pressure is one-dimensional and a re-invoke would still return the same data, so the redundancy is wasteful but coherent — weaker signal-to-noise than B's failure-mode trap. A's pilot validation is a real point in its favor, but B's design quality and richer discrimination ceiling outweigh the unverified pilot."}
